Smartphones and mobile learning for teac: This domain examines how smartphones are adopted and used by emergent and rural users in the Global South, including studies of usage patterns in India and the untapped potential of mobile learning in Ghana's secondary education. It explores the feasibility of introducing mobile technology into schools, such as Kenya's Competency Based Curriculum, and the challenges of integrating smartphones into teacher support programs.
AI/ML mapping of educational access and: This domain applies machine learning and satellite imagery to map educational access and socio-economic conditions, including large-scale school mapping through weakly supervised deep learning and temporal prediction of socio-economic indicators. It addresses identifying education deserts and 'last mile' school access in developing countries, as well as broader frameworks for mapping vulnerability among disadvantaged urban populations.
AI tutoring and child-computer interacti: This domain covers intelligent tutoring systems like AutoTutor and the use of generative AI to support marginalized and rural learners, including applications for equity between rural and urban areas in Brazil. It also examines child-computer interaction in the Global South and specialized AI tools such as sign language interpretation for Nigerian children.
Digital and data literacy measurement fo: This domain focuses on developing and validating measures of digital literacy specifically for populations with limited internet experience. It also addresses conceptualizing data literacy as an essential competency for participating in an increasingly data-driven society.
One-device-per-learner hardware initiati: This domain covers initiatives that provide individual computing devices to learners, centered on the One Laptop Per Child (OLPC) mission's principles of child empowerment and the XO laptop's design for learning. It also considers sustainable approaches to hardware access, such as reusing landfill-bound computers to bridge the digital divide.
Teacher-centered technology support and: This domain examines how teachers collaborate and are supported in technology use, including insights into teacher collaboration in West Kenya and design-based research bridging teacher aspirations and goal-setting. It also incorporates systematic review evidence on the factors affecting ICT use in classrooms by teachers.
Women's empowerment through educational: This domain explores technologies designed to empower rural and village women in developing countries, including participatory video and audio platforms using DVDs and IVR systems and smartphone-based empowerment. It features context-specific applications such as vocational education for seaweed farmers in Tamil Nadu and a user-centered seasonal calendar app for women in India.
System-scale education reform and equity: This domain addresses large-scale education reform and equity efforts, including Teaching at the Right Level programs, barefoot movements, and bold plans to reach millions of out-of-school girls in India. It also engages methods for ensuring fairness and stability in access to education and strategies for fixing broken education systems without additional funding.
ICT integration in resource-constrained: This domain examines the integration of ICT in resource-limited educational settings, including mobile cloud remote learning platforms, digital content platforms for sustainable education and disaster resilience, and the constraining and enabling factors of ICT use in rural Nepali schools. It also covers digital technology for sustainable development and cybersecurity concerns in resource-constrained South African schools.
